entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cultivated species Cicer arietinum L. used in improvement programs

entities.name: WILD CICER SPECIES
entities.description: Annual wild species of the genus Cicer used for chickpea improvement and conservation

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD
entities.description: The duration of light exposure which affects plant phenology and growth

entities.name: VERNALIZATION
entities.description: Temperature sensitivity that can hinder the regeneration and utilization of wild Cicer species

entities.name: EXTENDED PHOTOPERIOD TREATMENTS
entities.description: Application of 15, 18, 21, and 24 hours of light to study plant response

entities.name: PHENOLOGY
entities.description: The timing of biological events in the plant life cycle

entities.name: GROWTH
entities.description: The physical development and increase in size of the plant

entities.name: DAYS TO FIRST FLOWERING
entities.description: The number of days from planting until the first flower appears

entities.name: YIELD-RELATED TRAITS
entities.description: Characteristics that influence the overall productivity of the plant

entities.name: VEGETATIVE PHASE
entities.description: The period of plant growth prior to the transition to flowering

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE PHASE
entities.description: The period of plant growth involving flowering and seed production

entities.name: POD NUMBER
entities.description: The number of pods produced per plant, an agronomic trait

entities.name: SEED YIELD PER PLANT
entities.description: The total seed production per plant, an agronomic trait

entities.name: REGENERATION
entities.description: The process of renewing plant materials for conservation in genebanks

entities.name: HYBRIDIZATION
entities.description: The process of crossing different species or varieties to create hybrids

entities.name: C. RETICULATUM
entities.description: A wild Cicer species that responds best to an 18h photoperiod for regeneration and reducing vegetative phase

entities.name: C. JUDAICUM
entities.description: A wild Cicer species that responds best to a 15h photoperiod

entities.name: C. YAMASHITAE
entities.description: A wild Cicer species that responds best to a 15h photoperiod

entities.name: C. BIJUGUM
entities.description: A wild Cicer species responding to 15h for regeneration and 18h for reducing vegetative phase

entities.name: C. PINNATIFIDUM
entities.description: A wild Cicer species responding to 15h for regeneration and 18h for reducing vegetative phase

entities.name: C. CHORASSANICUM
entities.description: A wild Cicer species showing weak response to extended photoperiod treatments

entities.name: C. CUNEATUM
entities.description: A wild Cicer species showing weak response to extended photoperiod treatments
